questions.question_text: You are driving at 50 km/h, the same speed as the vehicle in front. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the condition of the roadway.
answers.2: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 50 km/h.
answers.3: I reduce my speed.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the risk of loose chippings.

questions.question_text: You are approaching the intersection where you are required to turn left. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the steering requirements of the truck.
answers.2: I remain in my own traffic lane up to the intersection.
answers.3: I pull slightly to the right in the traffic lane.
answers.4: I apply my brakes.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 40 km/h.
answers.3: I move my foot over to the brake pedal.
answers.4: I warn the children by a brief horn signal.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 40 km/h. What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of a railway crossing with barriers.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of a railway crossing without barriers.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of crossing traffic.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 50 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic sign.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.3: I continue at an unchanged speed of 50 km/h.
answers.4: I brake and look both ways.

questions.question_text: You have stopped by the roadside and are about to move off. There is no traffic in your blind spots. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of oncoming traffic.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of traffic approaching from behind.
answers.3: I move off immediately.
answers.4: I wait for the time being before moving off.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 80 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful that crossing traffic ahead has a duty to give way to all traffic.
answers.3: I hold back for traffic from the side roads.

questions.question_text: You are required to turn right at the intersection. There is no traffic behind you. How do you proceed?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic lights.
answers.2: I continue in my own traffic lane up to the intersection.
answers.3: If the lane is clear, I move into the traffic lane to my right.
answers.4: I continue in my own traffic lane a little longer.

questions.question_text: Which rules apply to the use of crawler lanes?

answers.1: I have a duty to use the crawler lane if I am driving more slowly than the speed shown on the traffic sign.
answers.2: If I am driving faster than the indicated speed, I have a duty to leave the crawler lane.
answers.3: When I drive into or out of the crawler lane, I follow the rule on changing traffic lanes.
answers.4: When I drive into or out of the crawler lane, I follow the merge rule.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 60 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic signs.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.3: It is prohibited to drive at more than 30 km/h through the bend.
answers.4: The maximum recommended speed through the bend is 30 km/h.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 40 km/h in a built-up area. What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of oncoming traffic.
answers.2: I hold back for oncoming traffic.
answers.3: Oncoming traffic must hold back for me.
answers.4: I reduce my speed and continue carefully further along the road.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 70 km/h and have caught up with the slowly moving grey passenger car. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I increase my speed and merge in in front of the grey passenger car.
answers.3: I reduce my speed slightly.
answers.4: I merge in behind the grey passenger car.

questions.question_text: You are required to continue straight ahead. What must you be particularly mindful of a little further ahead?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the tilting bridge further ahead.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the risk of strong side winds further ahead.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the risk of aquaplaning.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 20 to 30 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic sign.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of whether oncoming traffic is waiting.
answers.3: I stop and wait for oncoming traffic.
answers.4: I continue at an unchanged speed or increase it slightly.

questions.question_text: What is the highest speed permitted after the traffic sign?

answers.1: I may drive at a maximum of 80 km/h.
answers.2: I may drive at a maximum of 70 km/h.
answers.3: I may drive at a maximum of 60 km/h.
answers.4: I may drive at a maximum of 50 km/h.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 80 km/h and are required to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic sign.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful that there may be living creatures crossing the road.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful that there may be wild animals on the roadway.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 80 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 80 km/h.
answers.2: I apply my brakes.
answers.3: I pull over towards the solid side line on the right.
answers.4: I pull over and drive beyond the solid side line on the right.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 40 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 40 km/h.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the children.
answers.3: I stop at the stop line.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the sign for a single-track railway crossing.

questions.question_text: When do I have a duty to switch on the lights of the passenger car?

answers.1: I have a duty to use lights all day long.
answers.2: I have a duty to use lights from one hour before sunset until one hour after sunrise.
answers.3: I have a duty to use lights from sunset until sunrise.
answers.4: I have a duty to use lights from one hour after sunset until one hour before sunrise.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 30 km/h and would like to continue straight ahead. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 30 km/h.
answers.2: After the traffic sign, it is prohibited to drive at more than 15 km/h.
answers.3: After the traffic sign, the maximum recommended speed is 15 km/h.
answers.4: Parking outside the marked parking spaces is prohibited.

questions.question_text: You have driven into the intersection in order to turn left. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the traffic lights on the corner.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of oncoming traffic.
answers.3: I set off slowly in order to turn left.
answers.4: I wait a while longer in the middle of the intersection.

questions.question_text: You have stepped out of the white passenger car just outside a built-up area. According to the legal provisions, which of the following apply here?

answers.1: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for 2 to 3 minutes.
answers.2: I am permitted to park the passenger car here.

questions.question_text: What is defined as parking under the traffic rules?

answers.1: If I leave the passenger car stationary without a driver for 2 to 3 minutes, this is regarded as parking.
answers.2: If I leave the passenger car stationary for more than 3 minutes while I remain in the vehicle, this is regarded as parking.
answers.3: If I leave the passenger car stationary for more than 3 minutes without a driver, this is regarded as parking.
answers.4: If I leave the passenger car stationary for 10 to 20 minutes while loading or unloading baggage, this is regarded as parking.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 50 km/h. What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: After the traffic sign, it is prohibited to drive at more than 30 km/h.
answers.2: After the traffic sign, I should drive at least 30 km/h.
answers.3: After the traffic sign, the maximum recommended speed is 30 km/h.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful that I am driving into an area with speed suppression measures.

questions.question_text: What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful that I may only drive to the right.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful that I may only drive to the left.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful that I have a free choice as to whether I drive to the left or to the right.
